title: modiola prostrata
intro: Modiola prostrata, also known as creeping broomrape, is a perennial flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate various health issues, including colds, fever, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal benefits.
